issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .section-header p
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which is #aaaaaa (grey) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1, which fails WCAG AA requirements of 4.5:1 for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border, leaving button focus invisible to keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size with insufficient padding on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 36px (within 24–43px range) with vertical padding of only 6px (var(--space-1-5)), which is below the 8px compensation threshold, resulting in a touch target smaller than the recommended 44×44 px.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on footer navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer navigation links have min-height: 24px with no vertical padding specified, placing them at the minimum threshold without compensation, which may result in a touch target below the recommended 44 px.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-offset: -3px without restoring outline width, resulting in no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility class incorrectly exposes content to visual display
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and overflow: visible, which makes the content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually while keeping it accessible to screen readers. This defeats the purpose of the utility.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with zero padding, which is below the 24 px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24 px minimum touch target size, making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible selector
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only sets outline-color without outline width, resulting in no visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (for the :active state translateY effect), but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition property for hover/active effects, but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override to respect user motion preferences.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on headings
issuesFound.explanation: The h1–h6 elements use letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) which resolves to -0.025em, reducing readability for users with dyslexia or low vision by compressing character spacing.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .step-content h3
issuesFound.explanation: The step content heading uses letter-spacing: var(--letter-spacing-tight) (-0.025em), which can reduce readability for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Negative letter-spacing impairs readability on .features-section .section-header h2

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast for secondary text
issuesFound.explanation: The custom property --colour-text-secondary is set to #aaaaaa, which has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1 against white backgrounds (#ffffff). This fails the WCAG AA requirement of 4.5:1 for normal text, making content difficult to read for users with low vision.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px but provides no visible alternative such as box-shadow, border, or a non-zero outline value. Keyboard users navigating via Tab key will not see which button has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The base button class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. While padding increases the visual size, the minimum explicit dimension should be at least 44px to ensure adequate touch target area for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in footer navigation
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links use text-decoration: none and rely solely on colour (var(--colour-neutral-400)) to distinguish them from surrounding text, with no font-weight increase, border-bottom, or background highlight provided. Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot identify these as links.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width and height of 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended target size. With zero padding specified, the touch area is insufficient for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has an explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this small target.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector inherits outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset to -3px without setting a visible outline width, style, or alternative indicator. This results in no visible focus indicator for elements using this class, preventing keyboard users from tracking focus position.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets only outline-color while inheriting outline: none from the global reset, resulting in no visible focus ring because outline-width and outline-style are not specified. Keyboard users cannot identify focused elements using this modifier class.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below recommended size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended touch target size. The reduced size makes it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate the button accurately.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button includes transform in its transition property (transform var(--transition-fast)) to create a press effect on :active, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the movement.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button includes transform in its transition property for hover/active effects without a prefers-reduced-motion override. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the button transform animation, potentially causing vestibular issues.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative in footer bottom text

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users interacting with buttons, failing WCAG 2.4.7 Focus Visible.

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to visually hide content. Content meant for screen readers only will be visible on the page, cluttering the visual interface and confusing users.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer brand paragraph
issuesFound.explanation:
The .footer-brand > p selector uses color: var(--colour-neutral-400) (#94a3b8) against a dark background (var(--colour-neutral-900) which is #0f172a)? No, looking at the code, footer has background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) (line 1493). #94a3b8 on #0f172a is actually good contrast (light on dark). Wait, let me check: #94a3b8 is a light grayish color. #0f172a is very dark. Contrast should be good. Actually, looking at line 1497: `.footer-brand > p { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); ... }` and footer background is var(--colour-neutral-900). So this is light text on dark background. Contrast ratio: #94a3b8 vs #0f172a. Luminance of #94a3b8 is ~0.39, #0f172a is ~0.008. Contrast: (0.39+0.05)/(0.008+0.05) = 0.44/0.058 = 7.6:1. This passes. I made a mistake. The footer background is dark, not white. So this is not a violation. I should remove this issue.

Let me check other footer elements:
- `.footer-brand address` uses `var(--colour-neutral-500)` (#64748b) on dark background. Contrast should be OK.
- `footer nav a` uses `var(--colour-neutral-400)` on dark background. This is OK.

So issues 22, 23, 24 are invalid because the background is dark, not white. I need to remove them.

Let me check if there are any other contrast issues I missed on white backgrounds.
- `.hero-lead` uses `var(--colour-neutral-600)` (#475569) on white. Contrast ~6.3:1. OK.
- `.step-content p` uses `var(--colour-neutral-700)` (#334155). OK.
- `.faq-item dd p` uses `var(--colour-neutral-700)`. OK.
- `.newsletter-section` has gradient background, skip.
- `.contact-section > p` uses `var(--colour-text-secondary)`? No, line 1413: `color: var(--colour-text-secondary)` which is #aaaaaa on white? No, contact-section has background-color: var(--colour-bg-subtle) which is #f8fafc (very light gray). #aaaaaa on #f8fafc is even worse contrast. But line 1413 shows `.contact-section > p { ... color: var(--colour-text-secondary); ... }`. Yes, this is a violation.

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px, which is above the 24px minimum but below the 44px recommended size, and has no padding to compensate. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on how-it-works section paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .how-it-works > p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a light gray background (var(--colour-bg-subtle) is #f8fafc), resulting in insufficient contrast for normal text (18px font-size).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on products section header paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .products-section .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails the WCAG AA requirement for normal text (18px font-size).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on pricing section header paragraph
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-section .section-header p selector uses color: var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) against a light gray background (var(--colour-bg-subtle) is #f8fafc), resulting in insufficient contrast for normal text (18px font-size).

issuesFound.title: Account navigation links underlined by colour alone
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector sets text-decoration: none and provides only a colour change on hover (no border-bottom, font-weight change, or background highlight). This violates WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color, as links are distinguished from surrounding text only by colour.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links underlined by colour alone
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets text-decoration: none and provides only a colour change on hover. Without additional non-colour visual differentiators, users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish these links from regular text.

issuesFound.title: Social links underlined by colour alone
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector sets text-decoration: none and provides only a colour change on hover. Users with colour vision deficiencies cannot distinguish these links from surrounding text without an additional non-colour visual indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule-block. This removes the keyboard focus indicator for all elements in standard browsing modes, blocking access for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border in the same rule-block. This removes the keyboard focus indicator specifically for buttons, blocking access for keyboard users interacting with forms or actions.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: .btn

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a

issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. Elements intended for screen readers only will be visible to all users, cluttering the interface and confusing the visual layout.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector uses transform and transition properties to animate movement, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this animation for users who prefer reduced motion. issuesFound.fix: Line 696: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size for checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input has explicit width and height of 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (AA). This makes the checkbox difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1070: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The hero CTA button (.hero-ctas .btn-primary) includes transform in its transition but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion override specifically for this selector, potentially causing motion sickness for sensitive users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 760: (add after existing rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. While some specific links (like .feature-card a and .footer-bottom p a) provide underline on hover, the majority of navigation links (header, footer nav, account nav) rely solely on color to distinguish them from surrounding text, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.
issuesFound.fix: Line 125: a { text-decoration: underline; } (or provide border-bottom alternative for specific link classes)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset specified, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This is a failure of WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.
issuesFound.fix: Line 604: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with outline-offset: 0, removing the focus indicator entirely without providing a box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline replacement in the same rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 703: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown submenu links ([id$="-submenu"] li a) remove the outline on focus-visible with only a negative outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation within dropdown menus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 668: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset specified, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users activating the search button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 713: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible with only outline-offset specified, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation in the account menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 732: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link class includes transform in its transition property (transition: transform var(--transition-base)) but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3 Animation from Interactions.
issuesFound.fix: Line 293: (add after existing rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)) but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this spatial movement animation for users with vestibular disorders.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540: (add after existing rule) @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: background-color var(--transition-fast), border-color var(--transition-fast), color var(--transition-fast), box-shadow var(--transition-fast); } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image has a transition on transform (transition: transform var(--transition-slower), box-shadow var(--transition-slower)) and the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 797 is empty, providing no override to disable the parallax/transform motion for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 797: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; } .hero-image img:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card (.pricing-card--featured:hover) applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01) on hover, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 1085 only targets the base .pricing-card:hover, leaving the featured card transform animation active for users who prefer reduced motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1085: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .pricing-card:hover { transform: none; } .pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on skip link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip-link has background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) and color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.33:1. This is below the 4.5:1 minimum required for normal text (font-size: var(--font-size-base) = 16px), violating WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum).
issuesFound.fix: Line 293: .skip-link { ... background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); ... } (where --colour-brand-primary-hover: #004aaa provides 7.8:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on primary button
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class sets background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) and color: var(--colour-text-on-brand) (#ffffff), providing only 3.33:1 contrast ratio. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 for normal text (font-size: var(--font-size-base) = 16px) which requires 4.5:1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 549: .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); color: var(--colour-text-on-brand); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on recommended plan badge
issuesFound.explanation: The plan-recommended-badge has background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary) (#5588bb) and color: var(--colour-neutral-0) (#ffffff), with a contrast ratio of only 3.33:1. This is below the 4.5:1 required for the small text (font-size: var(--font-size-xs) = 12px), violating WCAG 1.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1106: .plan-recommended-badge { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); color: var(--colour-neutral-0); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on table "not included" indicators
issuesFound.explanation: The comparison table cells for "Not included" use color: var(--colour-neutral-300) (#cbd5e1) against a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.4:1. This is below the 3:1 required for large text (font-size: var(--font-size-xl) = 20px), making the content invisible to many users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1154: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"], .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not available"] { color: var(--colour-neutral-500); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative like a border or box-shadow in the same rule-block. This removes the keyboard focus ring for all elements, making navigation impossible for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 331: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px, but outline-offset alone does not create a visible ring. No box-shadow or border is provided in this rule-block.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible selector sets outline: none without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot identify focus on the mobile navigation toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 523: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the outline on focus without a replacement. Users navigating menus via keyboard will lose track of their position.
issuesFound.fix: Line 566: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a box-shadow or border in the same block. While :focus has a shadow, :focus-visible overrides it without replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 593: #search-input:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see when this button is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 609: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. This affects keyboard access to login/profile links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 626: nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px, which is below the 44px recommended size for touch targets. This makes buttons difficult to activate on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 417: .btn { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum. Small buttons are prone to mis-taps.
issuesFound.fix: Line 473: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-lg
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-lg class sets min-height: 36px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Large buttons should be easier to hit, not smaller.
issuesFound.fix: Line 481: .btn-lg { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-4) var(--space-8); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons need adequate space for touch.
issuesFound.fix: Line 488: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .nav-toggle
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle button sets min-height: 28px and min-width: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Mobile menu toggles are critical interaction points.
issuesFound.fix: Line 513: .nav-toggle { min-height: 44px; min-width: 44px; padding: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #nav-menu > li > a
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. This degrades the experience for touch users navigating the main menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line 543: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: [id$="-submenu"] li a
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links set min-height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Submenu items are often harder to hit accurately.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-3) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The search input sets height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Text inputs should be easy to tap to focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 577: #search-input { height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-3); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the 44px recommended size. Small submit buttons are difficult to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 603: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkboxes set width: 20px and height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Custom checkboxes must meet minimum size requirements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1371: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer links set min-height: 24px. While above the 24px minimum, they are below 44px and lack compensating padding in this rule.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1393: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, and clip: auto. This makes content fully visible instead of hiding it visually for screen readers only.
issuesFound.fix: Line 356: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class uses transition: transform, which causes spatial movement. The global @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at Line 667 is empty and does not disable this.
issuesFound.fix: Line 667: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-ctas .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary class uses transition: transform. No prefers-reduced-motion override exists to disable this movement for users who request it.
issuesFound.fix: Line 667: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing reduces space between words, making text difficult to read for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 313: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #94a3b8 / background #f8fafc ratio 1.67:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .logo-bar-label text colour (#94a3b8) on background (#f8fafc) has a contrast ratio of approximately 1.67:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.2:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .feature-card p text colour (#aaaaaa) on background (#ffffff) has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.2:1, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links (#nav-menu > li > a) have text-decoration: none. Hover state changes colour and background but does not add an underline or other non-colour indicator.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 545: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on nav menu items
issuesFound.explanation: The #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button selectors set min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Navigation links are hard to tap on mobile.
issuesFound.fix: Line 545: #nav-menu > li > a, #nav-menu > li > button { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The [id$="-submenu"] li a selector sets min-height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size. Dropdown menu items are difficult to select on touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 560: [id$="-submenu"] li a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on #search-input
issuesFound.explanation: The #search-input selector sets height: 28px, which is below the 44px recommended size for interactive form controls. The search field is hard to tap.
issuesFound.fix: Line 595: #search-input { height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] selector sets width: 28px and height: 28px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum. The search icon button is too small.
issuesFound.fix: Line 615: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] selector sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px absolute minimum. Checkboxes are difficult to toggle on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1220: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer nav links
issuesFound.explanation: The footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a selector sets min-height: 24px, which meets the absolute minimum but is below the 44px recommended size for easy interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1260: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search label.sr-only selector uses display: none, which completely removes the label from the accessibility tree. Screen reader users will not hear the search label.
issuesFound.fix: Line 590: #site-search label.sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link selector uses transition: transform, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 400 is empty and does not disable this motion. Users who prefer reduced motion will still see the slide animation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 400: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn selector uses transition: transform, but there is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this specific transform transition for buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 415: .btn { transition: none; } (Add within @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce))

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector uses transition: transform, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 725 is empty and does not disable the 3D rotation effect.
issuesFound.fix: Line 725: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which condenses words together. This significantly reduces readability for users with cognitive disabilities or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 175: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .btn-primary
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary selector uses #5588bb text on #ffffff background (via variables). The contrast ratio is approximately 3.7:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 440: .btn-primary { color: #003888; } (Or darken background to #004aaa)

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed without alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing a consistent non-colour alternative (like underline on hover) for all links. Users relying on non-colour cues cannot identify links.
issuesFound.fix: Line 206: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, :focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator in Windows High Contrast Mode. Box-shadow and outline-offset are suppressed in forced-colors mode, leaving users with no focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring for keyboard users. This makes it impossible for keyboard-only users to see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words and can make text difficult to read for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The selector #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible removes the outline and provides only outline-offset, resulting in no visible focus indicator for the search button when navigated via keyboard.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class incorrectly uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which makes the content visible to sighted users and potentially hides it from screen readers depending on implementation. It should use position: absolute with 1px dimensions and clipping to hide visually while keeping content in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"] has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with no padding, resulting in a 28×28px touch target below the 44px recommended size (and below the 24px minimum when accounting for the 0 padding).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on icon buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. Icon buttons are frequently used for critical actions and must meet minimum touch target requirements.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a selector inherits text-decoration: none from the global a rule, and its hover state at line 456 only changes color without adding underline, border-bottom, or other non-colour visual differentiator, making links distinguishable only by colour.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on FAQ buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .faq-item dt button:focus-visible removes the outline with only a negative outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring for the FAQ accordion buttons.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on form inputs
issuesFound.explanation: The selectors .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, and .form-group textarea:focus-visible remove the outline with no visible replacement, leaving keyboard users unable to see which form field is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The selector .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible removes the outline with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for custom-styled checkboxes.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on footer address text
issuesFound.explanation: The .footer-brand address uses color: var(--colour-neutral-500) which is #64748b against background-color: var(--colour-neutral-900) which is #1e293b, resulting in approximately 3.2:1 contrast ratio, failing the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on social links
issuesFound.explanation: The .social-links a selector inherits text-decoration: none, and its hover state at line 880 only changes colour, making the links indistinguishable from surrounding text for colour-blind users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on small buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkboxes
issuesFound.explanation: The .form-group input[type="checkbox"] has width: 20px, height: 20px, and min-height: 20px, all below the 24px minimum touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on product cards

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which makes content visible on screen instead of hiding it visually. It fails to hide content from sighted users while keeping it for screen readers, breaking the intended "screen-reader-only" functionality.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability

issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus without a visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .table-wrapper:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The scrollable table wrapper removes the outline on focus without a visible replacement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .faq-item dt button:focus-visible

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets (WCAG 2.5.8).

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is 28px × 28px with no padding, resulting in a touch target smaller than 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input is 20px × 20px, which is below the 24px minimum required for touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer nav links have min-height: 24px with no compensating padding defined in this rule, potentially falling below the 44px recommended size.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and clip: auto, which fails to hide content visually. It effectively makes screen-reader-only content visible to sighted users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transition: transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 483 is empty and does not disable this motion.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn element uses transition: transform. The @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block does not disable this motion for buttons.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transition: transform. The corresponding @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 843 is empty.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .comparison-section tbody td[aria-label="Not included"]
issuesFound.explanation: The text color #cbd5e1 on background #ffffff or #f8fafc has a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails WCAG 2.2 AA (requires 4.5:1 for normal text).

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-brand address a
issuesFound.explanation: The link color #94a3b8 on footer background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, failing WCAG 2.2 AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The link color #94a3b8 on footer background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, failing WCAG 2.2 AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .footer-bottom > p a
issuesFound.explanation: The link color #94a3b8 on footer background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, failing WCAG 2.2 AA.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .social-links a
issuesFound.explanation: The link color #94a3b8 on footer background #555555 has a contrast ratio of approximately 2.6:1, failing WCAG 2.2 AA.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have text-decoration: none and do not gain an underline on hover. They are distinguished from surrounding text by colour alone, which fails SC 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, the function toggles CSS classes and emits events but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state to users, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1852: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment in the open function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and adds CSS classes, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance function correctly sets it to "false", creating an asymmetry that prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2102: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` at the beginning of the _openInstance function.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() fails to set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item is expanded, the function sets item.open = true and animates the panel, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger to "true". The close() function correctly sets it to "false", breaking the accordion pattern for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3805: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` in the open function after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a form field as invalid, the function links an error element via aria-describedby but never sets aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as invalid programmatically, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and WCAG 3.3.1 Error Identification.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1201: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` in the markInvalid function before the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: inlineValidator._renderResult() propagates missing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When validation fails, the function calls srUtils.markInvalid which lacks aria-invalid support. The field receives error styling and aria-describedby linkage, but the critical aria-invalid attribute is never applied, leaving assistive technologies unaware of the invalid state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4508 (in else block): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` after calling `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl);`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu lacks live region announcements for open/close actions
issuesFound.explanation: When dropdown menus open or close, no announcement is made to screen readers via the liveRegion system. Users navigating with assistive technologies are unaware that content has appeared or disappeared, violating WCAG 4.1.3 Status Messages.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2108 (in _openInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown menu opened');`. Line 2142 (in _closeInstance): Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion._onKeydown() missing Enter/Space activation handlers
issuesFound.explanation: The keyboard handler manages Arrow keys, Home, and End for navigation between FAQ triggers, but does not handle Enter or Space keys to activate (toggle) the current item. Since faqRegistry.parse() explicitly assigns role="button" and tabindex="0" to non-button triggers, keyboard users cannot expand/collapse items without explicit Enter/Space handlers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3920: Add cases `case KEYS.ENTER:` and `case KEYS.SPACE:` that call `e.preventDefault(); toggle(items[current]);` and return.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() fails to activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: While createFocusTrap is instantiated during init and stored in _trap, the open() function never calls _trap.activate(). Keyboard focus is not confined to the mobile navigation drawer when open, allowing users to tab behind the overlay into the underlying page content.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1865: Add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();` after the `_lockScroll();` call in the open function.

fpTitles: inlineValidator._renderResult() propagates missing aria-invalid
faqAccordion._onKeydown() missing Enter/Space activation handlers

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When form validation marks a field as invalid, it links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Screen readers cannot identify the field as having an error.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In srUtils.markInvalid function, after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` add: `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute is not updated to "true". Screen readers will continue to announce the button as collapsed, preventing users from understanding that the menu is now open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.open function, after `_open = true;` add: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing dropdown menus changes the visible content but does not announce the state change to screen reader users. Users may not realize the dropdown has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In dropdownMenu._openInstance function, after setting aria-expanded add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown menu opened');` Line: In dropdownMenu._closeInstance function, after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` add: `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ accordion item expands, the trigger button's aria-expanded attribute remains "false" or unset. Screen readers cannot convey the expanded state to users, making it appear that the item is still collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In faqAccordion.open function, after `item.open = true;` add: `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: createScrollSpy._activate does not announce active section changes
issuesFound.explanation: As the user scrolls through the page, the active navigation link updates visually and via aria-current, but the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users may not know which section is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In createScrollSpy._activate function, after `addClass(link, cfg.activeClass);` add: `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.sectionActive || 'Section {section} active').replace('{section}', section.id || ''));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not move focus into the drawer
issuesFound.explanation: Upon opening the mobile navigation drawer, keyboard focus remains on the trigger button instead of moving to the drawer content. Keyboard users cannot immediately access the navigation links without manually tabbing forward.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.open function, before `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open'...` add: `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0] || _drawer; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus({ preventScroll: true });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not return focus to the trigger button
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer closes, focus is not returned to the trigger button that opened it. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order and must navigate back to the trigger manually.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.close function, after `_unlockScroll();` add: `if (_trigger) _trigger.focus({ preventScroll: true });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap is never initialized or activated
issuesFound.explanation: The _trap variable is declared but never assigned a createFocusTrap instance, and activate() is never called on it. Consequently, keyboard focus is not trapped within the open drawer, allowing users to tab behind the modal overlay.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In mobileNav.init function, after `_initialised = true;` add: `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });` Line: In mobileNav.open function, after `_lockScroll();` add: `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: productFilter filter tabs missing aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The category filter tabs function as a tablist but only toggle CSS classes. They lack aria-selected="true" on the active tab, preventing screen readers from identifying which filter is currently applied.
issuesFound.fix: Line: In productFilter._syncTabAriaStates function, inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);` add: `setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function within the `mobileNav` module toggles the navigation drawer visibility but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close()` function correctly sets it to "false", creating a state mismatch that confuses screen reader users about the menu's actual status.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1660: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the `open()` function after setting `_open = true`.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` function within the `mobileNav` module hides the navigation drawer but does not announce "Navigation menu closed" to the live region. The `open()` function includes this announcement, so the lack of symmetry leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1695: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');` before the function ends.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function misses ARIA state and announcement
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance()` function within the `dropdownMenu` module reveals the dropdown panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger and does not announce the change to the live region. This prevents screen reader users from knowing the dropdown is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1830: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` and `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');` inside the `_openInstance()` function.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance()` function within the `dropdownMenu` module hides the panel and updates `aria-expanded` to "false" but does not announce the closure to the live region. Users are not informed when the dropdown collapses.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1855: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');` before the function ends.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The `open()` function within the `faqAccordion` module expands the answer panel but does not set `aria-expanded="true"` on the question trigger button. The `close()` function sets it to "false", causing inconsistent state reporting for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2730: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` inside the `open()` function after setting `item.open = true`.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function does not announce state change
issuesFound.explanation: The `close()` function within the `faqAccordion` module collapses the answer panel but does not announce the action to the live region. The `open()` function announces expansion, so the missing collapse announcement creates an incomplete experience.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2775: Add `liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60)));` before the function ends.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function fails to set aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid()` utility function adds an error description via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the field itself. This means screen readers may not identify the field as invalid despite the error message being linked.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` inside the `markInvalid()` function.

mobileNav.close does not return focus to the trigger button
dropdownMenu _openInstance function misses ARIA state and announcement

srUtils markInvalid function fails to set aria-invalid

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class to the drawer but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The corresponding close function sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function closes the menu and updates aria-expanded but does not announce the "Navigation menu closed" state to screen readers via the live region, whereas the open function does announce. This creates an inconsistent experience for assistive technology users.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _openInstance function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The _closeInstance function sets aria-expanded="false", leading to incorrect state reporting for screen reader users.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu _closeInstance function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._closeInstance function closes the dropdown and updates aria-expanded but does not announce the state change to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technology are not notified when the dropdown closes.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function expands the answer panel but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the question trigger button. The close function sets aria-expanded="false", causing screen readers to incorrectly report the item as collapsed.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close function does not announce state change to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the answer panel and updates aria-expanded but does not announce the state change to screen readers. The open function announces expansion, so the close action should announce collapse for consistency.

issuesFound.title: srUtils markInvalid function fails to set aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to announce that a field contains an error.

issuesFound.title: pricing _syncToggleState uses aria-selected on buttons without role="tab"
issuesFound.explanation: The pricing._syncToggleState function sets aria-selected on billing period buttons. Unless these buttons have role="tab" in the HTML, aria-selected is ignored. Toggle buttons should use aria-pressed to indicate their active state.

issuesFound.title: Form validation not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The validation helper functions markInvalid and markValid only manage aria-describedby relationships to link error messages, but neither function sets the aria-invalid attribute to "true" when a field is invalid, nor clears it to "false" when the field becomes valid. Screen readers rely on aria-invalid to convey the validation state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line in markInvalid(): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');` Line in markValid(): `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');` (or `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');`)

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu instance open and close functions manage panel visibility using CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but fail to synchronize the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (inst.trigger). This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded or collapsed state of the dropdown menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _openInstance(): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` Line in _closeInstance(): `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When filtering products, the visible result count updates in the DOM, but the change is not announced to screen readers via a live region. Users relying on assistive technologies are not informed how many products match the active filter criteria.
issuesFound.fix: Line in _updateCountBadge(): `liveRegion.announce(`${count} products found`);` (or similar appropriate message)

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function not updating aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function sets item.open to true and reveals the answer panel, but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the item.trigger button. Although the close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", the omission in the open function leaves screen readers unaware that the content has expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: pricing period change not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing periods, the price values update visually but the change is not announced via a live region. Screen reader users may not realize that the displayed prices have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Line in setPeriod(): `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation trigger button is initialized without an aria-expanded attribute. Since the drawer is closed by default, the trigger should be initialized with aria-expanded="false" to communicate the initial state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line in init() after selecting _trigger: `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search combobox input is configured with aria-controls pointing to the suggestion listbox, but aria-expanded is not initialized to "false" when the component mounts and the suggestion list is hidden. This leaves the expanded state undefined for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line in mount() after setting aria-haspopup: `setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap not activated
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, the focus trap (_trap) is never activated, allowing keyboard focus to escape to focusable elements behind the drawer. Additionally, focus is not programmatically moved into the drawer when it opens, violating WCAG focus management requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix: Line in open(): `_trap.activate();` and `const firstFocusable = getFocusableChildren(_drawer)[0]; if (firstFocusable) firstFocusable.focus();`

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader hidden state not exposed to accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the header is hidden on scroll down via the hide() function, it is only visually hidden using a CSS class. If the CSS does not set display:none, the header remains in the accessibility tree and its focusable elements can still be tabbed to. The code does not set aria-hidden="true" to remove it from the accessibility tree.
issuesFound.fix: Line in hide(): `setAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden', 'true');` Line in show(): `removeAttr(_header, 'aria-hidden');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation open and close functions toggle the visibility of the drawer and body classes but fail to update the aria-expanded state on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Function mobileNav.open: _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');
  Function mobileNav.close: _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu instance functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu controller manages the hidden state of the panel but does not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button when opening or closing. The trigger state remains stale after the initial registration.
issuesFound.fix:
Function dropdownMenu._openInstance: inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');
  Function dropdownMenu._closeInstance: inst.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function sets the item state and reveals the panel but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets it to false, causing state desynchronization.
issuesFound.fix: Function faqAccordion.open: item.trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Assistive technologies may not announce the field as invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Function srUtils.markInvalid: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid utility function removes the error description but does not remove or reset the aria-invalid attribute to "false". The field may remain stuck in an invalid state for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Function srUtils.markValid: field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid'); // or field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters does not announce count changes to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The filter application updates the product count badge text content but does not announce this change to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when the number of results changes.
issuesFound.fix: Function productFilter.applyFilters: liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod does not announce price changes to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period toggle updates the price display text for all plan cards but does not announce the price change or period switch to an aria-live region. Users are not informed of the new pricing context.
issuesFound.fix: Function pricing.setPeriod: liveRegion.announce(`Prices switched to ${period} billing`);

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator does not announce number animation to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The statistic counter animation updates the text content of the number element dynamically but does not use an aria-live region to announce the final value or the update to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Function statCounterAnimator._animate: onComplete: () => { el.textContent = _format(cfg.target, cfg); liveRegion.announce(el.textContent); }

issuesFound.title: siteSearch suggestionListRenderer mount does not initialize aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The search suggestion listbox mount function sets aria-controls and role but does not initialize aria-expanded="false" on the input element. The initial state of the popup is undefined for assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Function suggestionListRenderer.mount: setAttr(input, 'aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init does not initialize aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation initialization finds the trigger element but does not set the initial aria-expanded="false" attribute. The button's state is unknown before the first interaction.
issuesFound.fix: Function mobileNav.init: _trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent trigger lacks aria-controls and aria-expanded management
issuesFound.explanation: The button or link with [data-cookie-prefs] that opens the consent banner does not have aria-controls pointing to the banner, nor is its aria-expanded state managed when the banner shows/hides.
issuesFound.fix:
Function cookieConsent._showBanner: const trigger = document.querySelector('[data-cookie-prefs]'); if(trigger) trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'true');
  Function cookieConsent._hideBanner: const trigger = document.querySelector('[data-cookie-prefs]'); if(trigger) trigger.setAttribute('aria-expanded', 'false');

issuesFound.title: searchResultsHighlighter highlight does not announce content changes
issuesFound.explanation: The search results highlighter modifies the DOM by injecting <mark> tags into result text but does not announce that results have been highlighted or updated to screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Function searchResultsHighlighter.highlight: liveRegion.announce(`${tokens.length} terms highlighted in results`);

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render does not announce chip list updates
issuesFound.explanation: The active filter chips list is dynamically updated (added/removed) based on filter state, but this change is not announced via an aria-live region. Users are unaware that filter chips have changed.
issuesFound.fix: Function activeFilterChips.render: liveRegion.announce(`${chips.length} active filters`);

issuesFound.title: productFilter syncPagination does not announce page changes
issuesFound.explanation: The pagination text is updated to reflect the current page number, but this change is not announced to screen readers. Users do not know they have moved to a new page of results.
issuesFound.fix: Function productFilter._syncPagination: liveRegion.announce(`Page ${current} of ${pages}`);
