issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid input. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1052 (inside markInvalid): add `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() fails to set aria-expanded="false" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is closed, the function removes CSS classes and hides the overlay but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button back to "false". Screen reader users are not informed that the menu has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2177 (inside close function, after _open = false): add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close() omits live-region announcement for screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The function closes the navigation drawer and returns focus, but unlike the open() function, it does not announce the state change via the live region. Users are not audibly notified that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2203 (before closing brace): add `if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function reveals the dropdown panel by removing the hidden attribute and adding CSS classes, but it never sets aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2254 (after inst.open = true): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes lack live-region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Neither the open nor close function announces the dropdown state change via the live region. While the visual change is apparent, screen reader users do not receive confirmation that the menu has opened or closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2259 (in _openInstance): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Dropdown opened');` Line 2285 (in _closeInstance): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Dropdown closed');`

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher.init() omits initial aria-pressed on toggle buttons
issuesFound.explanation: The function loads and applies the saved theme but does not synchronize the aria-pressed state of the theme toggle buttons to reflect the current selection. Buttons may appear unselected to screen readers until clicked.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4125 (after _apply(_resolve(_current))): add `qsa('[data-theme-toggle]').forEach(btn => { const btnTheme = btn.getAttribute('data-theme-toggle'); setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(btnTheme === _current)); });`

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod() omits live-region announcement of price changes
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is toggled, prices update visually with animation, but the new price information is not announced to screen readers. Users may not realize the prices have changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2890 (after updating plan cards): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.pricingChanged || `Prices updated to ${period} billing`);`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance() omits aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The function hides the dropdown panel but never sets aria-expanded="false" on the trigger button. The button continues to report an expanded state after the menu is closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2272 (after inst.open = false): add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters() omits live-region announcement of results
issuesFound.explanation: After filtering products, the function updates the count badge and empty state visually, but it does not announce the result count or "no results" status via the live region. Screen reader users are unaware that the filter has been applied or how many results are shown.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2695 (inside filterAnimation.run callback): add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` (or appropriate string for zero results)

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() omits aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is expanded, the function animates the panel open and updates internal state, but it never sets the aria-expanded attribute to "true" on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot report that the answer is visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3156 (after item.open = true): add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid() fails to clear aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a field becomes valid, the function removes the error description association but does not remove the aria-invalid attribute or set it to "false". The field continues to be reported as invalid to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1078 (inside markValid, after removing aria-describedby): add `removeAttr(field, 'aria-invalid');` or `setAttr(field, 'aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop._onClick() omits live-region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls and focus moves, but there is no auditory confirmation via the live region that the action succeeded. While focus movement helps, an explicit announcement confirms the action for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2362 (after setTimeout): add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.init() omits initial aria-expanded and aria-controls on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: During initialization, the trigger button is located but neither aria-expanded="false" nor aria-controls (pointing to the drawer) is set. The button lacks the necessary context to indicate it controls a collapsible region.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2232 (after _initialised = true): add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` and `if (_drawer.id) setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', _drawer.id);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.open` and `mobileNav.close` functions toggle the navigation drawer visibility but fail to update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `_trigger` button. Screen reader users will not know the current state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1330: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1355: setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav close action not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.close` function hides the navigation drawer but does not announce the state change to screen readers via the live region, unlike the `open` function which does. Users are not informed when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1355: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `mobileNav.init` function does not set the initial `aria-expanded` state on the `_trigger` button. If the menu is closed by default, the trigger should explicitly have `aria-expanded="false"`.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1280: if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` functions show/hide the panel but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the `inst.trigger` button. The accessible state remains out of sync with the visual state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1490: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');
  Line 1505: setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `dropdownMenu._openInstance` and `dropdownMenu._closeInstance` functions change visible content but do not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when a dropdown opens or closes.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1490: liveRegion.announce('Menu opened');
  Line 1505: liveRegion.announce('Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` function expands the answer panel but fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the `item.trigger`. The `close` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state management.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2655: setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `faqAccordion.open` and `faqAccordion.close` functions toggle visibility but do not announce the change to screen readers. Users are not informed when an answer expands or collapses.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 2655: liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} — answer expanded`);
  Line 2680: liveRegion.announce(`${item.question} — answer collapsed`);

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-selected state
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter._syncTabAriaStates` function toggles the visual active class on filter tabs but does not update the `aria-selected` or `aria-pressed` attribute. Screen reader users cannot identify the active filter.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2095: setAttr(btn, 'aria-selected', String(isActive));

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters result not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The `productFilter.applyFilters` function updates the product grid and count badge but does not announce the new result count to screen readers via an aria-live region.

issuesFound.explanation: The `pricing.setPeriod` function updates price displays and savings messages but does not announce the billing period change to screen readers. Users are not informed of the context switch.

issuesFound.explanation: The `suggestionListRenderer.mount` function sets up the combobox ARIA attributes but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` on the input element. The state is undefined until the first interaction.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When a form field is marked as invalid, the function adds an error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field itself. Without aria-invalid, screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users, even though the error message is associated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 900: Add setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true'); inside the markInvalid function before or after the addDescribedBy call.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function expands the FAQ answer panel and removes the hidden attribute, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. While the close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", the asymmetric implementation leaves screen reader users unaware when content is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2150: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); immediately after item.open = true; in the open function.

issuesFound.title: productFilter.applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied, the product grid updates and the count badge changes, but no announcement is made to screen reader users about the new result count or that filtering has completed. Users may not realize the content has updated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1950: Add liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`); or similar announcement after the filter animation completes and the count is updated.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is opened and closed via the open() and close() functions which manipulate CSS classes on the drawer and body, but neither function updates the aria-expanded attribute on the hamburger trigger button to communicate the visibility state to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1200: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true'); in the open function after _open = true;
  Line 1210: Add setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false'); in the close function after _open = false;

issuesFound.title: pricing.setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users toggle between monthly and annual billing periods, the prices update visually on the page, but the change is not announced to screen reader users. Users may be unaware that the pricing information has changed or what the new values are.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2050: Add liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly); after the prices have been updated in the setPeriod function.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state from a field, the function removes the aria-describedby reference to the error message but leaves aria-invalid="true" set on the field. This causes screen readers to continue announcing the field as invalid even after it has been corrected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 910: Add removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID); or setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false'); at the end of the markValid function.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The code references a focus trap variable (_trap) that is deactivated in the close function, but the open function does not show activation of the focus trap or programmatic movement of focus into the navigation drawer. Keyboard users cannot navigate within the opened menu, and focus is not returned to the trigger button when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1200: Add _trap.activate(); in the open function after opening the drawer, and ensure _trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, {returnFocus: _trigger}); is instantiated in init.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` and `close` functions in the `mobileNav` module toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer using CSS classes but fail to update the `aria-expanded` state on the trigger button (`_trigger`). Screen reader users will not be informed whether the menu is currently open or closed.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function `mobileNav.open`: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `addClass(_drawer, ...)`
  Line Function `mobileNav.close`: add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `removeClass(_drawer, ...)`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger aria-expanded not initialised at page load
issuesFound.explanation: The `init` function in `mobileNav` locates the trigger and drawer but does not set the initial `aria-expanded="false"` attribute on the trigger button. Assistive technologies will not know the initial state of the menu control.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function `mobileNav.init`: add `if (_trigger) setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` before event listeners are attached

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions do not update aria-expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` and `_closeInstance` functions toggle the visibility of dropdown panels but do not update the `aria-expanded` attribute on the associated trigger button. While `register` sets the initial state, dynamic toggling leaves the ARIA state stale.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function `dropdownMenu._openInstance`: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `inst.open = true;`
  Line Function `dropdownMenu._closeInstance`: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The `open` function in `faqAccordion` removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel and adds the open class, but it fails to set `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. The `close` function correctly sets it to false, creating an inconsistent state where opening an item does not announce expansion.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function `faqAccordion.open`: add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid does not set aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `markInvalid` helper function links the error message via `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"` on the input field. Screen readers rely on `aria-invalid` to announce that a field has an error state.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function `srUtils.markInvalid`: add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid does not clear aria-invalid on field
issuesFound.explanation: The `markValid` helper function removes the error description but does not remove or reset the `aria-invalid` attribute to "false". The field may remain marked as invalid in the accessibility tree even after the error is resolved.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function `srUtils.markValid`: add `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` inside the function body

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu opens or closes, the change in visible content is not announced to screen reader users via a live region. Users may be unaware that a menu has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function `dropdownMenu._openInstance`: add `liveRegion.announce('Menu expanded');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', ...);`
  Line Function `dropdownMenu._closeInstance`: add `liveRegion.announce('Menu collapsed');` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', ...);`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Expanding or collapsing FAQ items updates the DOM but does not trigger an announcement in the live region. Screen reader users are not informed that an answer has been revealed or hidden.
issuesFound.fix:
Line Function `faqAccordion.open`: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question));` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`
  Line Function `faqAccordion.close`: add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question));` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`

issuesFound.title: productFilter result count changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: When filters are applied and the product count updates, the change is not announced to screen reader users. They may not realize the grid content has changed or how many items are now visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function `productFilter.applyFilters`: add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} products found`);` inside the `filterAnimation.run` callback after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Switching between monthly and annual billing periods updates the visible prices but does not announce the change to screen reader users. Users may not be aware that the displayed prices have changed to reflect a different billing cycle.
issuesFound.fix: Line Function `pricing.setPeriod`: add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'month' ? STRINGS.pricingMonthly : STRINGS.pricingAnnual);` after `_updateSavingsMessage(period);`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid not setting aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid utility function links the error message to the field via aria-describedby but omits setting aria-invalid="true". Screen readers cannot convey to users that the field contains invalid input, which is essential for form accessibility.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markInvalid: Add `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'true');` immediately after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer open and close functions toggle CSS classes on the drawer and body elements but never update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, leaving blind users unaware of the current state.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.open: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `_open = true;`
  Line mobileNav.close: Add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `_open = false;`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When opening an FAQ item, the function sets item.open to true and animates the panel height, but unlike the close function (which sets aria-expanded to false), it never sets aria-expanded to true on the trigger button. Screen readers cannot announce that the answer is now expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line faqAccordion.open: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle functions missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu open and close instance methods manipulate the panel visibility by adding/removing CSS classes and the hidden attribute, but fail to set aria-expanded on the trigger button. Assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded state of the dropdown to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line dropdownMenu._openInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`
  Line dropdownMenu._closeInstance: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` immediately after `inst.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: productFilter applyFilters missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: After applying filters and updating the visible product count, the function emits a custom event but does not write to an aria-live region. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that filters have been applied or how many results are now showing.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.applyFilters: Inside the filterAnimation.run callback, after `_updateCountBadge(totalMatches);`, add `liveRegion.announce(`${totalMatches} product${totalMatches !== 1 ? 's' : ''} found`);`

issuesFound.title: productFilter view mode toggles missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The view mode toggle buttons (grid/list) have aria-pressed set only when clicked (_onViewToggle), but the initial state is not set on page load. Screen readers cannot convey which view mode is active initially.
issuesFound.fix: Line productFilter.init: After `delegate(document.body, '[data-view-mode]', 'click', _onViewToggle);`, add `qsa('[data-view-mode]').forEach(b => { setAttr(b, 'aria-pressed', String(b.getAttribute('data-view-mode') === _productFilterState.viewMode)); });`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The initialization function discovers the trigger and drawer elements but does not set the initial aria-expanded state to "false" when the navigation is closed on page load. Screen readers assume an indeterminate state until the user first toggles the menu.
issuesFound.fix: Line mobileNav.init: After `_initialised = true;`, add `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.EXPANDED, 'false');` and `setAttr(_trigger, ARIA.CONTROLS, srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid not clearing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The markValid function removes the error description association but does not remove or set to false the aria-invalid attribute. Fields remain marked as invalid in the accessibility tree after being corrected, confusing screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line srUtils.markValid: Add `removeAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID);` or `setAttr(field, ARIA.INVALID, 'false');` at the end of the function before the closing brace.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch combobox missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input acts as a combobox (role="combobox") but the _open and _close functions do not toggle aria-expanded to reflect whether the suggestion listbox is displayed. Screen readers cannot convey the popup state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line siteSearch._open: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `_searchState.open = true;`
  Line siteSearch._close: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `_searchState.open = false;`

issuesFound.title: testimonialsCarousel play button missing initial aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel play/pause button is created and event listeners are attached, but the initial aria-pressed state is not set to match the _playing variable. Screen readers cannot convey whether the carousel is currently auto-playing or paused on initial load.
issuesFound.fix: Line testimonialsCarousel.init: After `_initialised = true;`, add `if (_playBtn) setAttr(_playBtn, 'aria-pressed', String(_playing));`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav focus trap never activated
issuesFound.explanation: Although a _trap variable is declared for focus trapping, the open function never activates the focus trap (createFocusTrap is never called and _trap.activate() is missing). Keyboard users can tab out of the open mobile menu into the background page, violating WCAG focus containment requirements for modal dialogs.
issuesFound.fix:
Line mobileNav.init: After `_initialised = true;`, add `_trap = createFocusTrap(_drawer, { returnFocus: _trigger });`
  Line mobileNav.open: After `addClass(document.body, CSS.navOpen);`, add `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger does not update aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav module's open and close functions toggle the visibility of the navigation drawer via CSS classes but do not update the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button (_trigger). Screen reader users will not be informed of the menu's state change.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.fix: Line 1473: <IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handleNext} className="testimonials-track__next">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <article> element representing a pricing plan lacks an aria-labelledby attribute referencing the internal heading ID. This prevents screen reader users from quickly identifying the specific plan when navigating by landmarks or articles.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1279: <article aria-labelledby={headingId} className="pricing-card__article">

issuesFound.title: PlanCard CTA link missing specific accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The call-to-action link within the pricing plan card uses generic text (e.g., "Get started") without an aria-label that includes the specific plan name. When multiple such links exist on the page, they must be distinguishable by accessible name.
